mnemonics.title: CLOUD Metrics
mnemonics.content:
C - Cost efficiency
L - Latency measurement
O - Operational uptime
U - Utilization rates
D - Data throughput

Remember: CLOUD helps measure what's good

acronyms.usage: Contract defining service standards like 99.9% uptime between cloud provider and customer
Quantifiable metric used to evaluate success of cloud services (e.g., response time, uptime)
Reliability metric measuring average time between system failures in cloud infrastructure
Measure of storage device performance in cloud environments
Complete cost measurement including compute, storage, networking, and operational expenses
Monitoring technique that captures actual user interactions and performance data
Statistical measures where 95% or 99% of requests fall within specified latency

visualTips.visualization: Think of a clock face - 99.9% means only 8.76 minutes of downtime in a week (approx. 1 minute per day)
Use a fuel tank analogy - utilization shows how full the tank is; too empty wastes money, too full risks running out
Imagine an elastic band that stretches (scale up) and contracts (scale down) based on demand pressure
Horizontal = adding more machines side by side, Vertical = upgrading existing machine (bigger engine)

analogies.analogy: Like a hotel manager predicting room bookings - need enough rooms for guests but not too many empty rooms wasting money
Like a restaurant adding temporary staff during lunch rush and sending them home when it's quiet - only pay for what you need
Like an apartment building - multiple tenants share the same building infrastructure but have private living spaces
Like a car automatically adjusting its speed based on traffic - more power when needed, conserve fuel when possible
